structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm (50%)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if measured airflow < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Measured airflow < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0 → Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Low primary airflow alarm (50%) diagnostic rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm (70%)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0 → Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than  70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than  70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: a. If the measured airflow is less than  70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Low primary airflow alarm (70%) diagnostic rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low-Discharge Air Temperature Alarm (17°C / 30°F)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: parallel fan-powered VAV terminal unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if heating hot-water plant proven ON and DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Heating hot-water plant proven ON, DAT < (DAT setpoint - 17°C/30°F) for 10 minutes → Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. If heating hot -water plant is proven ON, and the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: b. If heating hot -water plant is proven ON, and the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: b. If heating hot -water plant is proven ON, and the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Low-DAT alarm (17°C) diagnostic rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low-Discharge Air Temperature Alarm (8.3°C / 15°F)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: parallel fan-powered VAV terminal unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if heating hot-water plant proven ON and DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Heating hot-water plant proven ON, DAT < (DAT setpoint - 8.3°C/15°F) for 10 minutes → Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If heating hot -water plant is proven ON, and the DAT  is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: a. If heating hot -water plant is proven ON, and the DAT  is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: a. If heating hot -water plant is proven ON, and the DAT  is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Low-DAT alarm (8.3°C) diagnostic rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low-Discharge Air Temperature Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Suppress low-DAT alarms for zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0 for hot-water reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Low-DAT alarms suppressed if Importance-Multiplier = 0 for hot-water reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its hot-water  reset T&R  control loop, low -DAT alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its hot-water  reset T&R  control loop, low -DAT alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
evidence_text: c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its hot-water  reset T&R  control loop, low -DAT alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
judge_reason: Low-DAT alarm suppression rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Suppress low airflow alarms for zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R loop.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Alarms suppressed if Importance-Multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
evidence_text: c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
judge_reason: Alarm suppression rule based on Importance-Multiplier.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband State: Primary Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband, active primary airflow setpoint is the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: a. The active primary airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint.
judge_reason: Deadband airflow setpoint rule from guideline.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating State: Primary Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In heating, active primary airflow setpoint is the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: a. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
judge_reason: Heating state primary airflow setpoint rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Parallel Fan Minimum Airflow Setpoint (Pfan-z)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Pfan-z is the lowest rate at which the fan will operate when it is turned on but has the lowest possible speed signal from the BAS.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Pfan-z is the lowest rate at which the fan will operate when it is turned on but has the lowest possible speed signal from the BAS.

evidence_quote: Pfan-z is the lowest rate at which the fan will operate when it is turned on but has the lowest possible speed signal from the BAS.
evidence_text: Pfan-z is the lowest rate at which the fan will operate when it is turned on but has the lowest possible speed signal from the BAS.
judge_reason: Parameter specification directly from guideline text.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling State: Airflow Setpoint Mapping to Cooling Loop Output
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The cooling-loop output maps the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active airflow setpoint is mapped from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint based on cooling-loop output.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.

evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
evidence_text: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
judge_reason: Cooling loop mapping sequence from guideline.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Active Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling maximum endpoint is Vcool-max in Occupied, Cooldown, Setup modes; 0 otherwise. Minimum endpoint is Vmin* in Occupied mode; 0 otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.8.4 Endpoints  as a Function of Zone Group  Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0

evidence_quote:
Table 5.8.4 Endpoints  as a Function of Zone Group  Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
evidence_text:
Table 5.8.4 Endpoints  as a Function of Zone Group  Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
judge_reason: Operational endpoints table from Section 5.8.4.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Electric Reheat Minimum Airflow Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: parallel fan-powered VAV terminal unit with electric reheat
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For electric reheat systems, ensure the parallel fan at minimum speed provides airflow exceeding the minimum required by the electric heater.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For systems with electric reheat, ensure that the minimum airflow provided by the parallel fan at minimum speed exceeds the minimum required airflow for the electric heater.

evidence_quote: For systems with electric reheat, ensure that the minimum airflow provided by the parallel fan at minimum speed exceeds the minimum required airflow for the electric heater.
evidence_text: For systems with electric reheat, ensure that the minimum airflow provided by the parallel fan at minimum speed exceeds the minimum required airflow for the electric heater.
judge_reason: Application guidance for electric reheat minimum airflow.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling State: Parallel Fan Control for Ventilation (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In Occupied Mode, with ASHRAE 62.1 ventilation, parallel fan starts when primary airflow < Voz - 0.5*Pfan-z, stops when > Voz. Fan airflow setpoint = Voz - current primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Cooling AND Occupied Mode AND ventilation standard ASHRAE 62.1-2016 AND primary airflow drops below (Voz - Pfan-z/2)
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Parallel fan starts; fan airflow setpoint = Voz - current primary airflow setpoint. Fan stops when primary airflow rises above Voz.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016 , in Occupied Mode  only, parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus one half of Pfan -z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the current primary airflow setpoint.

evidence_quote: c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016 , in Occupied Mode  only, parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus one half of Pfan -z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the current primary airflow setpoint.
evidence_text: c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016 , in Occupied Mode  only, parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus one half of Pfan -z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the current primary airflow setpoint.
judge_reason: Parallel fan control sequence for ventilation per ASHRAE 62.1.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling State: Supply Air Temperature Above Room Temperature Limits Airflow
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If supply air temperature from the air handler exceeds room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint must not exceed the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Cooling AND supply air temperature from air handler > room temperature
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint.

evidence_quote: 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint.
evidence_text: 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint.
judge_reason: Operational constraint on airflow setpoint when SAT exceeds room temperature.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband State: Parallel Fan Ventilation (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband with ASHRAE 62.1, parallel fan runs if active primary airflow setpoint < Voz; fan airflow setpoint = Voz - active primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Deadband AND ventilation standard ASHRAE 62.1-2016 AND active primary airflow setpoint < Voz
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Parallel fan runs; fan airflow rate setpoint = Voz - active primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016, parallel fan runs if the active primary airflow setpoint  is below Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the active primary airflow setpoint.

evidence_quote: c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016, parallel fan runs if the active primary airflow setpoint  is below Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the active primary airflow setpoint.
evidence_text: c. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1 -2016, parallel fan runs if the active primary airflow setpoint  is below Voz. Fan airflow rate setpoint  is equal to Voz minus the active primary airflow setpoint.
judge_reason: Deadband parallel fan ventilation sequence per ASHRAE 62.1.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Coil Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. Direct control from zone temperature loop is not acceptable.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone in heating mode (or whenever the coil is enabled)
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate heating coil to maintain DAT at DAT setpoint; do not control directly from zone temperature control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.8.5.4.  The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. (Directly controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).

evidence_quote: 5.8.5.4.  The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. (Directly controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).
evidence_text: 5.8.5.4.  The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint. (Directly controlling heating off zone temperature control loop is not acceptable).
judge_reason: Heating coil control sequence directly from guideline.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV Damper Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain measured primary airflow at the primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Always when zone is active (no specific state listed)
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate damper to match measured primary airflow to the active primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.8.5.5.  The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at the primary airflow setpoint.

evidence_quote: 5.8.5.5.  The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at the primary airflow setpoint.
evidence_text: 5.8.5.5.  The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at the primary airflow setpoint.
judge_reason: VAV damper modulation rule from guideline.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Alarm (Status Mismatch)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: parallel fan in VAV terminal unit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If fan status input differs from output command for 15 seconds after a change, generate alarm: commanded ON & status OFF → Level 2; commanded OFF & status ON → Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status input ≠ output command for 15 seconds after change; commanded ON status OFF → Level 2; commanded OFF status ON → Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.8.6.3.  Fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command after a period of 15 seconds after a change in output status.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

evidence_quote:
5.8.6.3.  Fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command after a period of 15 seconds after a change in output status.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
evidence_text:
5.8.6.3.  Fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command after a period of 15 seconds after a change in output status.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
judge_reason: Fan status mismatch alarm rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if fan is OFF and airflow sensor reading exceeds max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Fan OFF, airflow sensor > max(10% Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes → Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.8.6.4.  Airflow Sensor Calibration.  If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: 5.8.6.4.  Airflow Sensor Calibration.  If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: 5.8.6.4.  Airflow Sensor Calibration.  If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Airflow sensor calibration alarm rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Valve Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if valve position = 0% for 15 minutes and DAT exceeds AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F).
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Valve position 0% for 15 minutes, DAT > AHU SAT + 3°C (5°F) → Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.8.6.6.  Leaking Valve.  If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes , and DAT  is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: 5.8.6.6.  Leaking Valve.  If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes , and DAT  is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: 5.8.6.6.  Leaking Valve.  If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes , and DAT  is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Leaking valve alarm rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hot-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: parallel fan-powered VAV terminal unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send hot-water reset requests: 3 req if DAT < setpoint - 17°C/30°F for 5 min, 2 req if < setpoint - 8.3°C/15°F for 5 min, 1 req if HW valve >95% until <85%, else 0.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send specified number of requests upstream.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.8.8.3.  If There  Is a Hot -Water Coil, Hot -Water Reset Requests
a. If the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the DAT  is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the HW valve position is less than 85 %.
d. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
5.8.8.3.  If There  Is a Hot -Water Coil, Hot -Water Reset Requests
a. If the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the DAT  is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the HW valve position is less than 85 %.
d. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text:
5.8.8.3.  If There  Is a Hot -Water Coil, Hot -Water Reset Requests
a. If the DAT  is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the DAT  is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint  for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the HW valve position is less than 85 %.
d. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
judge_reason: Hot-water reset request sequence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating-Hot Water Plant Requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: parallel fan-powered VAV terminal unit with hot-water coil and heating hot-water plant
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send heating-hot water plant request: 1 request if HW valve position >95% until <10%, else 0.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 1 request until HW valve position < 10%.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.8.8.4.  If There Is  a Hot-Water Coil  and a Heating Hot -Water Plant , Heating -Hot Water Plant  Requests.
Send the heating hot -water plant that serves the zone a heating hot -water plant request  as follows:
a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%.
b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
5.8.8.4.  If There Is  a Hot-Water Coil  and a Heating Hot -Water Plant , Heating -Hot Water Plant  Requests.
Send the heating hot -water plant that serves the zone a heating hot -water plant request  as follows:
a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%.
b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text:
5.8.8.4.  If There Is  a Hot-Water Coil  and a Heating Hot -Water Plant , Heating -Hot Water Plant  Requests.
Send the heating hot -water plant that serves the zone a heating hot -water plant request  as follows:
a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%.
b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
judge_reason: Heating-hot water plant request sequence.
